name: debian-inspector

metadata.name: debian-inspector
metadata.summary: Utilities to parse Debian package, copyright and control files.

documentation:
# debian-inspector

Utilities to parse Debian package, copyright and control files

The Python package `debian_inspector` is a collection of utilities to parse
various Debian package manifests, machine readable copyright and control files
collectively known as the Debian 822 format (based on the RFC822 email format).

It was originally named "debut" as a portmanteau of DEBian and UTilities.

## Origin

This library is based on a heavily modified and remixed combination of original
code with code from other origins:

- python-deb-pkg-tools from @xolox for its handling of Debian packages and the
  parsing of versions, dependencies and other package-to-package relationship
  fields and for its ability to process the syntax of Debian versions and
  compare them. See <https://github.com/xolox/python-deb-pkg-tools>
- dlt (Debian license tools) from @agustinhenze for its check of the coverage of
  a license file Files sections and a lot of inspiration. See
  <https://github.com/agustinhenze/dlt/>
- PGPy from @SecurityInnovation and @Commod0re for its ability to remove a PGP
  signature from an email. dsc (Debian Source Control) files are typically
  PGP-signed and are email-like. See <https://github.com/SecurityInnovation/PGPy>
- python-dpkg by @TheClimateCorporation and @memory for its ability to process
  the syntax of Debian versions and to compare them according to the spec.
  See <https://github.com/TheClimateCorporation/python-dpkg>

## Why?

Why create this seemingly redundant library? The official python-debian tools
and several other utilities already provide similar capabilities!

On the surface this is correct, but there are several reasons for a new utility:

- Existing tools parse control files strictly. This library tries to be more
  flexible. For instance it can recognize and fix some almost correct copyright
  files that are not fully "machine readable" but close enough to the spec to be
  worthy of recovery.
- Several of these tools have to deal with legacy compatibility. We do not have
  such a need. For instance, the Python standard library email module and one
  line of code is enough to parse a Debian 822 file fields. I doubt that this
  feature was available in the standard library when python-debian was started
  but it is here now and this vastly simplifies the code.
- The focus of this library is to parse and inspect control files, not to emit
  and create them, so the code and tests can be much simpler. For instance,
  rather than using more complex case-insensitive dictionary keys while
  preserving case for Deb822-like objects, this library uses lower case keys
  throughout.
- The official python-debian library is GPL-licensed making it it hard to
  combine with Apache-licensed libraries. I tried to work out a licensing change
  for python-debian with all its authors such that it could be integrated in
  permissive-licensed Python tools. Even though most current maintainers and
  contributors were OK with that relicensing to a permissive or an LGPL license,
  I could not get a reply and agreement from some important legacy authors:
  therefore the relicensing was not possible.

## License

SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 AND BSD-3-Clause AND MIT

This software combines software from several origins with different licenses.
All of these licenses apply as all original files have been refactored and remixed
significantly:

```
Copyright (c) nexB Inc. and others.
Copyright (c) Peter Odding.
Copyright (c) The Climate Corporation (https://climate.com)
Copyright (c) Security Innovation, Inc
Copyright (c) Agustin Henze <tin@sluc.org.ar>
```

functions.name: debian_inspector.utils.find_debian_architecture
documentation:
Find the Debian architecture of the current environment.

Uses {func}`os.uname()` to determine the current machine architecture
(the fifth value returned by {func}`os.uname()`) and translates it into
one of the [machine architecture labels] used in the Debian packaging
system:

| Machine architecture | Debian architecture |
| -------------------- | ------------------- |
| `i686`               | `i386`              |
| `x86_64`             | `amd64`             |
| `armv6l`             | `armhf`             |

When the machine architecture is not listed above, this function falls back
to the external command `dpkg-architecture -qDEB_BUILD_ARCH` (provided by
the `dpkg-dev` package). This command is not used by default because:

1. deb-pkg-tools doesn't have a strict dependency on `dpkg-dev`.
2. The `dpkg-architecture` program enables callers to set the current
   architecture and the exact semantics of this are unclear to me at the
   time of writing (it can't automagically provide a cross compilation
   environment, so what exactly does it do?).

```
returns

The Debian architecture (a string like i386, amd64,
armhf, etc).

raises

 when the
dpkg-architecture program is not available or reports an
error.
```

[machine architecture labels]: https://www.debian.org/doc/debian-policy/ch-controlfields.html#s-f-Architecture
[more architectures]: https://www.debian.org/ports/index.en.html#portlist-released

methods.name: debian_inspector.coverage.Coverage.compute
documentation: Compute the coverage and update self.

Helper to compute the "coverage" of a copyright file "files" paragraph. This
is used to check if all files in a directory are referenced by a one of the
patterns in a "files" field.

functions.name: debian_inspector.package.get_nva
documentation:
Return a tuple of (name string, Version object, archictecture string or
None) parsed from the `filename` of .deb, .udeb, .orig or .debian archive..

functions.name: debian_inspector.package.match_relationships
documentation:
Validate that `package_archive` DebArchive satisfies all the relationships
of a `relationship_sets`. Return True if valid and False otherwise.
TODO: simplify me

functions.name: debian_inspector.package.find_latest_version
documentation:
Return the DebArchive package archive with the highest version number given
a `packages` list of  package filename strings or DebArchive instances.
All package items must have the same package name

Raise ValueError if a package filename is invalid or if the packages do not
have all the same package name.

functions.name: debian_inspector.package.find_latest_versions
documentation:
Return a mapping {name: DebArchive} where DebArchive is the package archive
with the highest version number for a given package name given a `packages`
list of  package filename strings or DebArchive instances.

classes.name: debian_inspector.package.DebArchive

methods.name: debian_inspector.package.DebArchive.from_filename
documentation:
Parse the filename of a Debian binary package archive and return a
DebArchive instance. Raise ValueError if the `filename` is not valid.

methods.name: debian_inspector.package.DebArchive.to_tuple
documentation:
Return a tuple of name, Version, architecture suitable for sorting.
This tuple does not contain the original_filename value.
A .deb binary package archive.

classes.name: debian_inspector.package.CodeArchive

methods.name: debian_inspector.package.CodeArchive.from_filename
documentation:
Parse the `filename` of a Debian original package archive and return an OrigArchive instance.
Raise ValueError if the `filename` is not valid.

methods.name: debian_inspector.package.CodeArchive.to_tuple
documentation:
Return a tuple of name, Vresion, architecture suitable for sorting.
This tuple does not contain the original_filename values.

A Debian .orig or .debian package archive.
These are not architecture-specific.
For instance in ./pool/main/a/apr-util there are files such as:

- apr-util_1.5.4.orig.tar.bz2 that contains the original upstream code
- apr-util_1.6.1-4.debian.tar.xz that contains the Debian patches and
  control files

classes.name: debian_inspector.package.CodeMetadata
documentation:
A .dsc, copyright or changelog file present in the debian
package/metadata archive and contains package information
on the filename or as file contents.

For example in ./changelogs/main/d/diffutils/ there are
files such as:
- diffutils_3.7-5_copyright
- diffutils_3.7-5_changelog
And in .pool/main/b/base-files/ there are files such as:
- base-files_11.1+deb11u8.dsc

modules.name: debian_inspector.deb822
documentation:
A parser for deb822 data files as used by Debian control and copyright files,
and several related formats.

For details, see:
- https://www.debian.org/doc/debian-policy/ch-controlfields
- https://www.debian.org/doc/packaging-manuals/copyright-format/1.0/
- https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc2822/

Why yet another Debian 822 parser?

This module exists because no existing parser module supports some must-have
features. Neither the standard email module nor the python-debian library, nor
other existing libraries can do these:

- track the start and end line numbers of each field within each paragraph: we
  need line numbers to trace license detection in copyright files.

- lenient parsing recovering from common errors: this helps handling a larger
  variety of copyright files even if they are not exactly well-formed
Copyright (c) nexB Inc. and others. All rights reserved.
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
See http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 for the license text.
See https://github.com/nexB/debian-inspector for support or download.
See https://aboutcode.org for more information about nexB OSS projects.

functions.name: debian_inspector.deb822.get_paragraphs_as_field_groups
documentation:
Yield lists of Deb822Field for each paragraph in a `text` each separated
by one or more empty lines. Raise Exceptions on errors.

functions.name: debian_inspector.deb822.get_paragraphs_as_field_groups_from_file
documentation:
Yield lists of Deb822Field for each paragraph in a control file at
`location`. Raise Exceptions on errors.

functions.name: debian_inspector.deb822.get_paragraphs_as_field_groups_from_lines
documentation:
Yield lists of Deb822Field for each paragraph in a `numbered_lines` list
of NumberedLine. Raise Exceptions on errors.

functions.name: debian_inspector.deb822.clean_fields
documentation: Clean and return a `fields` list of Deb822Field.

classes.name: debian_inspector.deb822.NumberedLine

methods.name: debian_inspector.deb822.NumberedLine.is_field_declaration
documentation:
Return True if this is a continuation line.

For example:

```pycon
>>> NumberedLine(1, 'foo').is_field_declaration()
False
>>> NumberedLine(1, '').is_field_declaration()
False
>>> NumberedLine(1, '   ').is_field_declaration()
False
>>> NumberedLine(1, '  Some: bar ').is_field_declaration()
False
>>> NumberedLine(1, 'Some:').is_field_declaration()
True
>>> NumberedLine(1, 'foo: bar').is_field_declaration()
True
>>> NumberedLine(1, 'foo:bar').is_field_declaration()
True
>>> NumberedLine(1, 'foo: ').is_field_declaration()
True
>>> NumberedLine(1, ' .').is_field_declaration()
False
>>> NumberedLine(1, '    .').is_field_declaration()
False
```

methods.name: debian_inspector.deb822.NumberedLine.is_field_continuation
documentation:
Return True if this is a field declaration line.

For example:

```pycon
>>> NumberedLine(1, 'foo').is_field_continuation()
False
        >>> NumberedLine(1, '').is_field_continuation()
        False
        >>> NumberedLine(1, '   ').is_field_continuation()
        False
        >>> NumberedLine(1, '   ').is_field_continuation()
        False
>>> NumberedLine(1, '   foo').is_field_continuation()
True
>>> NumberedLine(1, ' .').is_field_continuation()
True
>>> NumberedLine(1, '   .').is_field_continuation()
True
>>> NumberedLine(1, ' "').is_field_continuation()
True
>>> NumberedLine(1, ' (').is_field_continuation()
True
```

methods.name: debian_inspector.deb822.NumberedLine.lines_from_text
documentation: Return a list of Line from a ``text``
A text line that tracks its absolute line number. Numbers start at 1.

classes.name: debian_inspector.deb822.Deb822Field

functions.name: debian_inspector.deps.parse_depends
documentation:
Return an AndRelationships from a Debian package relationship declaration
line. Raise ValueError when parsing fails.

`relationships` is a string containing one or more comma separated package
relationships or a list of strings with package relationships.

The input has a form such as `python (>= 2.6), python (<< 3)`, i.e. a
comma-separated list of expressions.

Each expression is a package name/versions/arch constraint or a pipe-
separated list of alternative.

functions.name: debian_inspector.deps.parse_alternatives
documentation:
Return a Relationship for an expression string that contains one or more
alternative relationships. Raise ValueError when parsing fails.

An expression with alternative is something such as as `python2.6 |
python2.7.`, i.e. a list of relationship expressions separated by `|`
tokens.

Each pipe-separated sub-expression is parsed with `parse_relationship()`

functions.name: debian_inspector.deps.parse_relationship
documentation:
Parse an expression containing a package name and optional version and
architecture constraints.

```
param expression

A relationship expression (a string).

returns

A  object.

raises

 when parsing fails.
```

This function parses relationship expressions containing a package name and
(optionally) a version relation of the form `python (>= 2.6)` and/or an
architecture restriction (refer to the Debian policy manual's documentation
on the syntax of relationship fields for details).
<https://www.debian.org/doc/debian-policy/ch-relationships.html>

methods.name: debian_inspector.deps.AbstractRelationship.names
documentation: A set of package names (strings) in the relationship.

methods.name: debian_inspector.deps.AbstractRelationship.matches
documentation:
Check if the relationship matches a given package name and version.

Return True, False or None:
  - True if the name and version match,
  - False if the name matches but not the version
  - None if the name does not match

A simple package relationship referring only to the name of a package.

classes.name: debian_inspector.deps.VersionedRelationship

methods.name: debian_inspector.deps.VersionedRelationship.matches

A conditional package relationship that refers to a package and certain
versions of that package.

methods.name: debian_inspector.deps.MultipleRelationship.names

classes.name: debian_inspector.deps.OrRelationships

methods.name: debian_inspector.deps.OrRelationships.matches
documentation:
Return True if at least one of the relationships match a given package
name and version strings.

Return False if at least one of the relationships name are matched
without the version or None otherwise.
A multi relationship where one of the relationships must be satisfied.

classes.name: debian_inspector.deps.AndRelationships

methods.name: debian_inspector.deps.AndRelationships.matches
documentation:
Return True if all the the relationships match a given package name and
version strings.

Return False if at least one relationship matches to False or None
otherwise.
A multi relationship where all relationships must be satisfied.

functions.name: debian_inspector.version.eval_constraint
documentation:
Evaluate a versions constraint where two Debian package versions are
compared with an operator such as < or >. Return True if the constraint is
satisfied and False otherwise.

functions.name: debian_inspector.version.compare_versions_key
documentation: Return a key version function suitable for use in sorted().

functions.name: debian_inspector.version.compare_strings_key
documentation: Return a key string function suitable for use in sorted().

functions.name: debian_inspector.version.compare_strings
documentation:
Compare two version strings (upstream or revision) using Debain semantics
and return one of the following integer numbers:

    - -1 means version1 sorts before version2
    - 0 means version1 and version2 are equal
    - 1 means version1 sorts after version2

functions.name: debian_inspector.version.compare_versions
documentation:
Compare two Version objects or strings and return one of the following
integer numbers:

  - -1 means version1 sorts before version2
  - 0 means version1 and version2 are equal
  - 1 means version1 sorts after version2

functions.name: debian_inspector.version.coerce_version
documentation:
Return a Version object from value.

```
param value

The value to coerce (a string or  object).

returns

A  object.
```

functions.name: debian_inspector.version.compare_version_objects
documentation:
Compare two Version objects and return one of the following
integer numbers:

  - -1 means version1 sorts before version2
  - 0 means version1 and version2 are equal
  - 1 means version1 sorts after version2

functions.name: debian_inspector.version.get_digit_prefix
documentation: Return the digit prefix from a list of characters.

functions.name: debian_inspector.version.get_non_digit_prefix
documentation: Return the non-digit prefix from a list of characters.

variables.name: debian_inspector.version.logger
documentation:
Parse, compare and sort Debian package versions.

This module is an implementation of the version comparison and sorting algorithm
described at
https://www.debian.org/doc/debian-policy/ch-controlfields.html#s-f-Version

This has been substantially modified and enhanced from the original python-dpkg
Dpkg class by Nathan J. Meh and team from The Climate Corporation and
code from python-deb-pkg-tools by Peter Odding to extract only the subset that
does the version parsing, comparison and version constraints evaluatiob.


So much so that little of this code may still looks like the original.

Some examples:

#### Compare two arbitrary version strings

    >>> from debian_inspector import debver
    >>> debver.compare_versions('0:1.0-test1', '0:1.0-test2')
    -1
    >>> debver.compare_versions('1.0', '0.6')
    1
    >>> debver.compare_versions('2:1.0', '1:1.0')
    -1

#### Use Version as a key function to sort a list of version strings

    >>> from debian_inspector.debver import Version
    >>> sorted(['0:1.0-test1', '1:0.0-test0', '0:1.0-test2'] , key=Version.from_string)
    ['0:1.0-test1', '0:1.0-test2', '1:0.0-test0']

classes.name: debian_inspector.version.Version
documentation:
Rich comparison of Debian package versions as first-class Python objects.

The {class}`Version` class is a subclass of the built in {class}`str` type
that implements rich comparison according to the version sorting order
defined in the Debian Policy Manual. Use it to sort Debian package versions
from oldest to newest in ascending version order like this:

> ```pycon
> >>> from debian_inspector.version import Version
> >>> unsorted = ['0.1', '0.5', '1.0', '2.0', '3.0', '1:0.4', '2:0.3']
> >>> print([str(v) for v in sorted(Version.from_string(s) for s in unsorted)])
> ['0.1', '0.5', '1.0', '2.0', '3.0', '1:0.4', '2:0.3']
> ```

This example uses 'epoch' numbers (the numbers before the colons) to
demonstrate that this version sorting order is different from regular
sorting and 'natural order sorting'.

functions.name: debian_inspector.contents.parse_contents
documentation:
Return a mapping of {path: [list of packages]} and a mapping of
{package: [list of paths]} from parsing a Debian Contents file at
`location`.
The Contents file are typically gzipped but we also accept plain text files.

If `has_header` is True, the file is expected to have a header narrative
and a FILE/LOCATION columns headers before the table starts in earnest.

See <https://wiki.debian.org/DebianRepository/Format#A.22Contents.22_indices>
for format details.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.as_formatted_lines
documentation:
Return a text formatted for use in a Debian control file with proper
continuation for multilines.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.as_formatted_text
documentation:
Return a text formatted for use in a Debian control file with proper
continuation for multilines.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.from_formatted_text
documentation:
Return cleaned text from a Debian formatted description text
using rules for handling line prefixes and continuations.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.from_formatted_lines
documentation:
Return text from a list of `lines` strings using the Debian
Description rules for handling line prefixes and continuations.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.collect_files
documentation:
Return a mapping of {name: File} from a Debian data mapping.

Note: the Files and Checksums-* fields have the same structure and
contain redundant data.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.collect_file
documentation:
Yield tuples of (name, size, digest) given a Debian "Files" value string
which contains digest, size and name.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.get_paragraphs_data_from_file
documentation:
Yield paragraph data mappings from the Debian control file at `location`
that contains multiple paragraphs (e.g. Package, status, copyright file,
etc.).

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.split_in_paragraphs
documentation:
Yield paragraphs from a `text` string that contains one or more paragraph
separated by empty lines. Each paragraph is a string.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.get_paragraphs_data
documentation:
Yield paragraph data mappings from the Debian control `text` string that
contains multiple paragraphs (e.g. Package, status, copyright file, etc.).

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.get_paragraph_data_from_file
documentation:
Return paragraph data from the Debian control file at `location` that
contains a single paragraph (e.g. a dsc file).

Optionally remove a wrapping PGP signature if `remove_pgp_signature` is
True.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.get_paragraph_data
documentation:
Return paragraph data from the Debian control `text`. The paragraph data is
an ordered mapping of {name: value} fields. If there is data that is not
parsable or not attached to a field name, this will be added to a field
named "unknown".

If there are duplicates field names, the string values of duplicates field
names are merged together with a new line in the first occurence of that
field.

Optionally remove a wrapping PGP signature if `remove_pgp_signature` is
True.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.line_separated
documentation: Return a list of values from a `value` string using line as list delimiters.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon._splitter
documentation:
Return a list of values from a `value` string using `separator` as list delimiters.
Empty values are NOT returned.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.space_separated
documentation:
Return a list of values from a `value` string using one or more whitespace
as list items delimiter. Empty values are NOT returned.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.read_text_file
documentation: Return the content of the file at `location` as text or None.

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.load_control_file
documentation:
Load a control file and return the parsed control fields.

```
param control_file

The filename of the control file to load (a string).

returns

A dictionary created by .
```

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.parse_control_fields
documentation:
Return an ordered mapping from parsing an`input_fields` mapping of Debian
control file fields. This applies a few conversions such as:

- The values of the fields that contain dependencies are parsed
  into Python data structures.

- The value of some fields such as `Installed-Size` from a string to a
  native type (here an integer).

functions.name: debian_inspector.debcon.normalize_control_field_name
documentation:
Return a case-normalized field name string.

Normalization of control file field names is not really needed when reading
as we lowercase everything and replace dash to underscore internally, but it
can help to compare the parsing results to the original file while testing.

According to the Debian Policy Manual field names are not case-sensitive,
however a conventional capitalization is most common and not using it may
break hings.

http://www.debian.org/doc/debian-policy/ch-controlfields.html#s-controlsyntax

classes.name: debian_inspector.debcon.FieldMixin

methods.name: debian_inspector.debcon.FieldMixin.attrib
documentation: Return an attrib class

methods.name: debian_inspector.debcon.FieldMixin.dumps
documentation: Return a string in Debian format for this field.
Base mixin for attrs-based fields.

classes.name: debian_inspector.debcon.SingleLineField
documentation: https://www.debian.org/doc/packaging-manuals/copyright-format/1.0/#single-line

documentation: https://www.debian.org/doc/packaging-manuals/copyright-format/1.0/#line-based-lists

classes.name: debian_inspector.debcon.LineAndSpaceSeparatedField
documentation: This is a list of values where each item is itself a space-separated list.

classes.name: debian_inspector.debcon.AnyWhiteSpaceSeparatedField
documentation:
https://www.debian.org/doc/packaging-manuals/copyright-format/1.0/#white-space-lists
This is a list of values where each item is itself a space-separated list.

classes.name: debian_inspector.debcon.FormattedTextField
documentation:
https://www.debian.org/doc/debian-policy/ch-controlfields#description
Like Description, but there is no special meaning for the first line.

classes.name: debian_inspector.debcon.DescriptionField

methods.name: debian_inspector.debcon.DescriptionField.dumps
documentation: Return a string representation of self.

https://www.debian.org/doc/debian-policy/ch-controlfields#description
5.6.13. Description

classes.name: debian_inspector.debcon.FilesField
documentation: This is a list of FileField

classes.name: debian_inspector.debcon.Debian822

methods.name: debian_inspector.debcon.Debian822.__init__
documentation:
Build a new instance from `data` that is either a file-like object with
a read() method, a text, a sequence of (key/values) or a mapping. Note
that the keys are always lowercased.

methods.name: debian_inspector.debcon.Debian822.dumps
documentation:
Return a text that resembles the original Debian822 format. This is not
meant to be a high fidelity rendering and not meant to be used as-is in
control files.

A mapping-like class that corresponds to a single deb822 paragraph like a
whole .dsc file.

See https://github.com/nexB/debian-inspector for support or download.

functions.name: debian_inspector.copyright.is_year_range
documentation: Return True if `text` is a year range.

functions.name: debian_inspector.copyright.is_machine_readable_copyright
documentation: Return True if a text is for a machine-readable copyright format.

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.LicenseField.has_doc_reference
documentation:
Return True if this license contains a reference to a Debian shared
license file in the /usr/share/common-licenses directory.

classes.name: debian_inspector.copyright.CopyrightStatementField
documentation:
Conventionally (but not in the spec) each line in a copyright is a space-
separated tuple of (year range, holder). If it cannot be parsed, the holder
contains all text.
This field represents one line, e.g. one statememt.

classes.name: debian_inspector.copyright.CopyrightField
documentation:
This represents a single "Copyright:" field which is a plain formatted text
but is conventionally a list of copyrights statements one per line

classes.name: debian_inspector.copyright.MaintainerField
documentation:
https://www.debian.org/doc/debian-policy/ch-controlfields#s-f-maintainer
5.6.2. Maintainer

classes.name: debian_inspector.copyright.BaseParagraph

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.BaseParagraph.get_field_names
documentation: Return a list of field names defined on this paragraph.

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.BaseParagraph.get_field_line_numbers
documentation: Return a tuple of (start_line, end_line) for the `field_name` field.

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.BaseParagraph.get_first_last_line_numbers
documentation: Return a tuple of (first line, last line) number of this paragraph.

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.BaseParagraph.from_fields
documentation:
Return a paragraph built from a list of Deb822Field.
If `all_extra` is True, treat all data as "extra_data".

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.BaseParagraph.is_empty
documentation: Return True if all fields are empty
Base paragraph with line numbers tracking.

classes.name: debian_inspector.copyright.CatchAllParagraph

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.CatchAllParagraph.is_all_unknown
documentation:
Return True if this is an "unknown" field.
We use the "unknown" field name for things that do not have a name.

A catch-all paragraph: everything is fed to the extra_data. Every field is
treated as formatted text.

classes.name: debian_inspector.copyright.CopyrightHeaderParagraph
documentation:
The header paragraph.

https://www.debian.org/doc/packaging-manuals/copyright-format/1.0/#header-paragraph

classes.name: debian_inspector.copyright.CopyrightFilesParagraph

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.CopyrightFilesParagraph.is_empty
documentation: Return True if this is empty.

A "files" paragraph with files, copyright, license and comment fields.

https://www.debian.org/doc/packaging-manuals/copyright-format/1.0/#files-paragraph

classes.name: debian_inspector.copyright.CopyrightLicenseParagraph

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.CopyrightLicenseParagraph.is_empty
documentation:
Return True if this is empty (e.g. was crated only because of a
'License:' empty field.

A standalone license paragraph with license and comment fields, but no files.

https://www.debian.org/doc/packaging-manuals/copyright-format/1.0/#stand-alone-license-paragraph

classes.name: debian_inspector.copyright.DebianCopyright

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.DebianCopyright.from_fields_groups
documentation:
Return a DebianCopyright from a `fields_groups` list of list of
Deb822Field.

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.DebianCopyright.get_header
documentation: Return the header paragraph or None.

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.DebianCopyright.merge_contiguous_unknown_paragraphs
documentation:
Update self.paragraphs, merging contiguous unknown-only
CatchAllParagraph paragraphs in one.

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.DebianCopyright.fold_contiguous_empty_license_followed_by_unknown
documentation:
Update self.paragraphs, such that a CatchAllParagraph paragraph with
"unknown" as license text is merged into a preceding empty (e.g. without
any text) CopyrightLicenseParagraph paragraph.

methods.name: debian_inspector.copyright.DebianCopyright.is_valid
documentation:
Return True if this is a valid Debian Copyright file.
If `strict` is True, validate strictly against the spec.

A machine-readable debian copyright file.
See https://www.debian.org/doc/packaging-manuals/copyright-format/1.0/
